stimulus: It is generally assumed that scientific discovery is a forward-moving process, in which earlier theories are supplanted by newer and more accurate ______blank the recent revival of interest in classical heliocentric models (among researchers reconstructing premodern astronomical methods) reminds us that ostensibly outdated frameworks may be revisited to illuminate present-day puzzles.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is the coordination of main clauses within a sentence. A semicolon conventionally joins the first main clause (“It is...accounts”) and the second main clause (“the recent...puzzles”). Placing the semicolon after “accounts” indicates that the supplementary phrase “even so” modifies the following clause, producing the most logical reading.

Choice A is incorrect because placing the semicolon after “so” attaches “even so” to the first clause, which is illogical. Choice B is incorrect because it results in a run-on sentence. Choice D is incorrect because, without a conjunction following it, a comma can’t join the two main clauses (comma splice).
